4375	  an \(A\)-linear map
4376	  \[
4377	    \mathrm{toCoverEqLocus} : \Gamma(\top, M)
4378	      \longrightarrow \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes})
4379	      = \{\, t \in \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i,M) : \mathrm{leftRes}(t) = \mathrm{rightRes}(t)\,\}.
4380	  \]
4381	\end{definition}
4382	
4383	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4384	[Global sections as an \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-module equalizer locus]
4385	  \label{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus}
4386	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaTopEquivEqLocus}
4387	  \uses{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus, def:fbcb_gammaModA, def:fbcb_gammaResA, def:fbcb_leftRes,
4388	        def:fbcb_rightRes, lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
4389	  % NOTE: bijectivity is established by the ELEMENT-LEVEL sheaf axioms
4390	  % (lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib for injectivity, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib for
4391	  % surjectivity) on the underlying Ab-sheaf M.presheaf; it does NOT route through the
4392	  % sheaf-condition-fork limit (lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib / gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork).
4393	  For \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\) and any open cover \(U\) of \(X\) (\(\bigvee_i U_i = \top\)), the
4394	  corestriction \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) of Definition~\ref{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus} is an
4395	  \(A\)-linear isomorphism
4396	  \[
4397	    \Gamma(X, M) = \Gamma(\top, M) \;\cong\; \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes}),
4398	  \]
4399	  identifying the global sections, as a module over the ground ring \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\), with
4400	  the submodule on which the two restriction legs agree.
4401	  \begin{proof}
4402	    \uses{def:fbcb_toCoverEqLocus, lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib,
4403	          lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
4404	    The map \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) is \(A\)-linear by construction, so it suffices to prove it
4405	    bijective on underlying sets. \emph{Injectivity:} if two global sections \(s, t\) have equal images,
4406	    their restrictions to every \(U_i\) agree, so by sheaf separatedness
4407	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_eq_of_locally_eq_mathlib}) applied to the underlying abelian-group sheaf
4408	    \(M.\mathrm{presheaf}\), \(s = t\). \emph{Surjectivity:} an element of the locus is a family
4409	    \((s_i)_i\) with \(\mathrm{leftRes} = \mathrm{rightRes}\), i.e.\ a compatible family on the cover; by
4410	    the gluing axiom (Lemma~\ref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) it amalgamates to a global
4411	    section restricting to \((s_i)_i\). Hence \(\mathrm{toCoverEqLocus}\) is an \(A\)-linear bijection,
4412	    so an isomorphism.
4413	  \end{proof}
4414	\end{lemma}
4415	
4416	\begin{lemma}\leanok
4417	[Flat base change commutes with the \texorpdfstring{$H^0$}{H0} equalizer]
4418	  \label{lem:fbcb_baseChangeGammaEquiv}
4419	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.baseChangeGammaEquiv}
4420	  \uses{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
4421	  Let \(B\) be a flat \(A\)-algebra (\(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\)). Then base changing the global
4422	  sections along \(A \to B\) is the equalizer of the base-changed restriction legs: there is a
4423	  \(B\)-linear isomorphism
4424	  \[
4425	    B \otimes_A \Gamma(X, M) \;\cong\;
4426	      \operatorname{eqLocus}\bigl(\mathrm{id}_B \otimes \mathrm{leftRes},\ \mathrm{id}_B \otimes \mathrm{rightRes}\bigr).
4427	  \]
4428	  \begin{proof}
4429	    \uses{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus, lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
4430	    Tensoring the keystone isomorphism Lemma~\ref{lem:fbcb_gammaTopEquivEqLocus} by \(B\) over \(A\)
4431	    gives \(B \otimes_A \Gamma(X,M) \cong B \otimes_A \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes},\mathrm{rightRes})\),
4432	    and by flatness of \(B\) over \(A\) the Mathlib equivalence \(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\)
4433	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}) carries \(B \otimes_A (-)\) of the equalizer to the
4434	    equalizer of the base-changed legs. Composing the two yields the stated \(B\)-linear isomorphism.
4435	    By the same presentation applied to the pulled-back sheaf, the right-hand side is the global sections
4436	    of \(M\) over the base-changed scheme, so this is the module-level core of the \(H^0\)
4437	    flat-base-change comparison.
4438	  \end{proof}
4439	\end{lemma}

The blueprint's `\begin{proof}` for `lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_conj` describes:

2. Split the composite into single-pair conjugate factors via `conjugateEquiv_symm_comp`.

This is accurate as a mathematical route but **does not specify** that the conjugate pair's right adjunction requires a named depth-3 composite (`keystoneAdjR`) or that the right-adjoint comparison nat-iso requires a named non-monolithic gluing (`keystoneBeta`). The three **major** findings do not trigger the must-fix-this-iter gate (no fake statements, no signature mismatches, no excuse-comments, no unauthorized axioms). They are blueprint coverage gaps: the helpers exist and typecheck, but are undocumented in the chapter. Blocking status: these should be addressed before `_legs_conj` closes, so the blueprint can guide the two-stage assembly proof.
